title: Introducing Claude Tag
description: Anthropic launched Claude Tag, a persistent AI agent that lives inside Slack and can be tagged into any channel to plan and execute tasks autonomously. Unlike the previous Claude Slack app, Claude Tag is multiplayer—one shared Claude instance collaborates with all members in a channel, maintaining context across conversations. Available in beta to Enterprise and Team customers, Anthropic reports that 65% of their own product team's code is already written by their internal version of Claude Tag.

title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of 'Illicitly' Accessing AI Models
description: Anthropic publicly accused Chinese technology giant Alibaba of orchestrating a large-scale campaign to illicitly access its Claude AI models through thousands of fraudulent accounts. The alleged effort specifically targeted Claude's most prized capabilities, including software engineering and agentic reasoning, with Anthropic tracing the activity to operators linked to Alibaba's Qwen AI lab. The accusations represent a significant escalation in cross-border AI intellectual property disputes between US and Chinese AI companies.

title: AI researchers continue to leave Google for its rivals
description: TechCrunch reports that Google DeepMind's talent exodus is deepening, with four senior researchers departing within the same week: Noam Shazeer (Gemini co-lead and Transformer co-author) joining OpenAI, Nobel laureate John Jumper (AlphaFold) joining Anthropic, and now Jonas Adler and Alexander Pritzel—both key Gemini contributors—also headed to Anthropic. Alphabet shares fell roughly 5–6% as investor confidence in Google's ability to retain frontier AI talent wavered.

title: Google Poised to Lose Two More High-Profile AI Staffers to Anthropic
description: Bloomberg reports that Jonas Adler, who worked on AI coding within Google's Gemini team, and Alexander Pritzel, who was involved in training AI systems, are both set to join Anthropic. The departures follow the announcements of Noam Shazeer and John Jumper leaving for OpenAI and Anthropic respectively, compounding concerns about Google's ability to maintain its position at the frontier of AI development.

title: Explore the markets with Interactive Brokers and Grok
description: xAI announced the integration of Grok with Interactive Brokers, bringing AI-powered analysis directly into clients' trading workflow. The integration enables portfolio analysis, scenario modeling, market research, and conversational order instructions without requiring a new account. Interactive Brokers clients can connect their existing accounts to Grok in minutes at no additional cost.

title: Grok on Databricks
description: xAI announced that Grok models are now available on Databricks, allowing enterprises to access Grok's AI capabilities within their existing data and AI workflows. The integration enables organizations to build and deploy Grok-powered applications using their proprietary data without moving it outside their environment.
